extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter bridged solo to PORTE Richie's attack in final 800m, held off MATTHEWS Michael and MARTIN Dan despite unclipping mid-sprint on the steep finish.
tactical_patterns: PORTE Richie attacked 800m out on steep finish attempting to steal time on GC rivals, forcing SAGAN's early bridge.
BMC rode dual leadership with VAN AVERMAET Greg protected for sprint while PORTE attacked for GC seconds simultaneously.
SAGAN bridged alone then soft-pedaled before re-accelerating when chasers arrived, weaponizing momentum management on steep gradient.
BROWN Nathan targeted low-value KOM points early when pure sprinter teams controlled, trading green jersey workload for polka dots.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: Unclipped pedal mid-sprint on steep finish yet still held off world-class chasers, suggesting massive power reserve
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: Attacked 800m out on uphill finish hunting GC seconds, showing willingness to gamble despite Sky controlling yellow
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: Kept fourth despite BMC prioritizing PORTE's GC attack over pure sprint setup, revealing tactical flexibility
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Second place on steep finish confirms climbing sprint ability superior to pure fastmen like KITTEL who abandoned
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Third on steep finish from Quick-Step shows Quick-Step supporting his stage hunting not just pure sprint trains
rider_signals.BROWN Nathan: positive: Opportunistically harvested KOM points on minor climbs while sprinter teams controlled, efficient polka dot strategy in debut Tour
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: Retained yellow by staying attentive at steep finish where gaps opened, avoiding time trial losers' trap
